title: Part-Time Bookkeeper (QuickBooks Online) – Remote
description:
Part-Time Bookkeeper (QuickBooks Online) – Remote



# About the Role



I am a U.S.-based business owner looking for a **reliable part-time bookkeeper** to help maintain my books in **QuickBooks Online**.



This is a **long-term position for the right person**.



This is **not an accountant role**. I already work with an accountant. Your responsibility is to **keep the books organized, accurate, and updated weekly** so everything is ready for financial reporting and tax preparation.



---



# Responsibilities



• Categorize transactions in QuickBooks Online

• Reconcile bank accounts and credit cards

• Track business expenses

• Record client payments

• Create and send invoices when needed

• Maintain organized financial records

• Prepare books so they are ready for the accountant

• Generate basic monthly reports (Profit & Loss and expense summaries)



Books should be **reviewed and updated weekly** to keep records current.



---



# Requirements



• Experience using **QuickBooks Online**

• Basic understanding of bookkeeping principles

• Ability to **reconcile accounts accurately**

• Strong attention to detail

• Good written English

• Reliable internet connection

• Ability to work independently with minimal supervision



Bonus if you have experience working with **U.S. small businesses**.



---



# Hours & Pay



Approximately **5 hours per week**, depending on workload.



Pay is **up to $5 per hour depending on experience**.



---



# How to Apply



Start your message with the phrase:



**QBO Ready**



Applications that do not include this phrase will not be considered.



Then include:



• Your experience using **QuickBooks Online**

• How long you have worked as a **bookkeeper**

• Your availability each week



---



# Quick Skill Question



Please answer this question in your application:



A bank statement shows a balance of **$10,245**.

QuickBooks shows a balance of **$10,120**.



There is a **$125 difference**.



What are **three things you would check first** to find the issue?

title: Customer Support Manager (DTC Supplement Brand)
description:
FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM, EXCLUSIVE ROLE ONLY

No part-time work, agency setups, or side commitments.



We are a fast-growing supplement brand looking for a Customer Support Manager who is genuinely obsessed with efficiency and continuous improvement. This is not a role for someone who just wants to maintain the status quo or follow a manual. We need a leader who looks at a process and immediately thinks of three ways to make it faster, smarter, and better.

You will be responsible for building and scaling our support operations. We want someone who takes deep pride in ownership, turns chaos into systems, and ensures our customer experience is a competitive advantage. If you are a builder who thrives on fixing broken workflows and implementing high-level strategy, we want to talk to you.



What You Will Do



* Optimize & Implement: You will be the architect of our CS roadmap, constantly auditing and improving systems to ensure we stay ahead of our growth.

* System Ownership: Beyond just managing tickets, you will create, improve, and maintain our entire library of SOPs, macros, and internal documentation.

* Problem Identification: You will proactively identify patterns in customer friction and work across departments to solve issues at the root.

* Tech Stack Management: Take full ownership of our tools (Gorgias, Shopify, etc.), ensuring all automations, rules, and integrations are running at peak performance.

* Accountability: Own the department KPIs (CSAT, Resolution Time, Efficiency) and use data to drive every decision you make.

* Hiring: Hire, promote, and remove underperformers to maintain a high-output, accountable team culture

* Lead by Example: Set the standard for communication and problem-solving, eventually leading the hiring and training as the team scales.



Qualifications



* 5+ years leading customer service teams of 15+ in a fast-paced eCommerce environment

* Obsession with Improvement: You must be able to demonstrate how you have personally improved a system, workflow, or department in a previous role.

* Shopify Expert: Strong hands-on experience with Shopify and its ecosystem of apps.

* Tool Mastery: Advanced proficiency in Gorgias, Zendesk, or similar platforms, specifically in building out automations and complex workflows.

* Strategic Thinker: Ability to translate raw data into actionable process changes.

* Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English with the ability to lead with authority and clarity.

* Commitment: Willingness to commit full-time and exclusively to our brand for the long term.



This Role is Not for You If:



* You view this as "just a job" rather than something you take personal pride in building

* You need step-by-step direction and shy away from strategic decisions

* You're uncomfortable holding people accountable or addressing underperformance

* You present problems instead of solving them

* You want a comfortable, predictable 9-to-5 job



Click here to apply : 
/jobs/
customer-support-manager

title: Video Editor (DTC eCommerce)
description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing health and wellness brand obsessed with customer psychology and creative excellence. We don't just "make content," we build high-performance assets that drive millions in revenue. We are looking for a Video Editor who is genuinely passionate about performance, speed, and continuous improvement.



This is not a role for someone who just wants to follow a storyboard and clock out. We need a creative who looks at a hook and immediately thinks of three ways to make it more engaging, faster, and higher-converting.



Our philosophy: we prioritize 10 ads at 80% quality over 2 ads at 100%. Speed and volume win in performance marketing. If you're someone who obsesses over pixel-perfect polish at the expense of output, this isn't the right fit.



The Role

You will own the creation of high-volume, high-performance video ads, targeting 10–20 videos per week depending on length. This includes short-form for Meta/TikTok, long-form VSLs, AI avatars, B-roll edits, and voiceover-driven content.



You'll work from creative briefs and scripts provided by our creative strategists and copywriters, turning direction into polished, high-converting assets at speed. You take deep pride in ownership, turn raw footage and AI outputs into systematic creative machines, and ensure our creative output is a competitive advantage.



If you are a builder who thrives on iterating based on data rather than just "vibes," we want to talk to you.



What You Will Do

* Full-Spectrum Production: Create high-converting short-form ads (15–60s), UGC-style content, and long-form VSLs for Meta, TikTok, and YouTube.

* AI Mastery: Take full ownership of our AI tech stack, including Veo 3, HeyGen, Runway, and ElevenLabs for video, and Nano-Banana for statics, to produce high-quality AI video, avatar, and voiceover outputs at scale.

* Source Fresh Creatives: Proactively find and source fresh B-roll and visual assets from across the internet. You never run dry, and you never recycle.



What We're Looking For

Experience & Skills

* 2+ years of video editing experience, with specific experience editing for DTC brands or performance marketing.

* Technical mastery: Fluency in Premiere, Final Cut, or CapCut, plus advanced proficiency in AI video tools (Veo 3, HeyGen, Runway, ElevenLabs, Nano-Banana).

* Strategic thinking: Strong instincts for hooks, pacing, and storytelling in a Direct Response (DR) context.

* Resourcefulness: Proven ability to source fresh creatives, B-roll, and visual assets independently. You always have new material to work with.



Work Style

* Speed over perfection. You can move fast and deliver high volume without sacrificing the quality that matters.

* Implement feedback quickly, often same-day turnaround.

* Improvement-driven: You can demonstrate how you've personally improved a creative's performance through iteration or better editing workflows.

* Excellent English, clear written and verbal communication for seamless collaboration.

* Self-learner. You figure things out on your own. Whether it's a new tool, a new technique, or a new platform, you don't wait to be taught.

* Organized file structures and version control for rapid iteration.



Commitment

Full-time and exclusive. This role requires your full professional focus. We invest heavily in our people, training, tools, growth, and we're looking for someone who's equally invested in building something long-term with us.



Click here to apply :

Scaling winning ads and campaigns aggressively.



Identifying and killing underperforming creatives quickly.



Optimizing CPA and ROAS to maintain profitable growth.



Building structured testing and scaling systems.

Developing offer-driven advertising angles



This Role Is NOT For



? Someone who only sets up tracking

This Role IS For



? Someone with a sales-obsessed mindset

title: Backend Engineer - $3k project.
description:
We’re building a cold
 SaaS and need a smart, resourceful backend developer who can take our proof-of-concept and turn it into a production-grade system.



This is a $3,000 paid project to prove mutual fit, with the option to continue on ongoing projects or transition into full-time.



The core idea: a user enters their VPS SSH details (or can provision a VPS via OVH api or similar, and we add a margin on) -> our system automatically installs a mail server stack, configures domains & DNS, and creates cold
 inboxes ready to send.



It must be developed in typescript/node. 



???? What You’ll Do

- Rewrite and productionize an existing Node.js/TypeScript codebase (currently ~80% done)

- Install & configure mail server stack (Postfix, Dovecot, SSL/TLS) on Ubuntu VPS.

- Implement DNS automation (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, MX) via Cloudflare API.

- Create inboxes, balance across multiple IPs, and manage SMTP/IMAP routing.

- Build secure API endpoints for domain + inbox management.

- Add health monitoring (blacklist checks, server health, deliverability).



? Requirements

- Strong backend development experience with Node.js + TypeScript.

- Solid understanding of Linux server administration (Ubuntu).

- Familiarity with mail technologies (Postfix, Dovecot, SMTP, IMAP) is a big plus — or willingness to learn quickly.

- Experience with APIs, error handling, and production-ready code.

- GitHub profile or portfolio link required.



???? Compensation

- $2,000 USD for production-ready delivery of the initial project.

- Option to continue with ongoing projects or transition to full-time role with excellent pay + benefits.



???? How to Apply

- Send your GitHub (or portfolio link).

- Briefly describe a project where you managed servers, mail, or APIs. Or where you picked up technologies you didn't previously know, and worked with them.



Answer this question:

- How would you configure Postfix to send mail using multiple IPs? (short explanation is fine).



?? Applications without a GitHub link and question answer will be ignored.



Why Work With Us?

- We’re a fast-growing SaaS with real customers and exciting technical challenges.

- Flexible hours, remote-first.

- We pay well for quality work and value long-term relationships.



???? Apply today and show us why you’re the right person to build production-ready
 infrastructure.

title: Executive Assistant – Marketing & Operations - $1200 per month
description:
We're looking for someone TECHNICAL and ORGANISED with marketing experience who can write perfect English.



You need to be the type of person who's OCD about organization - someone who sees chaos and immediately needs to turn it into order.



---



$1200/mo, with pay-rises

Monday-Saturday 9am to 6pm UK time





**The Core Truth About This Role:**



We need someone who becomes INDISPENSABLE. Not replaceable. Not "good enough." Indispensable.



If you're just another person doing tasks i give them, and nothing more, then you aren’t indispensable (we can find 100s of people like this). But if you become the person who takes our chaos and turns it into systems - someone we literally can't run the business without - you'll be compensated like it.



As the founder, i want to focus on the ideas, the high-level, the strategy? i want to be able to send you a 10 minute voice note on a new idea and ask you to build out a test to prove if it’s worthwhile (maybe a cold
 campaign, or a landing page split test idea), then you go away, come back to me with a plan on how to test it, build it out, and test it.



This is taking my CHAOS and turning it into ORDER.



**Critical: You Must Be RELIABLE**



If you're someone who's not very organized, stop reading now. This won't work.



When I tell you "We need this done every Monday at 6pm," it happens every Monday at 6pm. Forever. Without me ever mentioning it again.



I shouldn't need to ask "Did you do that thing?" or "Can you get me this?" If I have to follow up with you, you're causing more problems than you're solving.



I tell you something ONCE. You put it in your calendar. It gets done. Every single time.



If I say "We need this report every Friday," I never have to ask about that report again. It just appears. Every Friday. Without fail.



That's the level of reliability we need.



**The Chaos Reality:**



As a startup, we're pure chaos. One week I'm telling you to build a content system and you need to learn that entire skill. Next week we're pivoting to something completely different and you're learning again.



This is stressful. This is difficult. This is not for everyone.



We need someone RESILIENT who thrives in this environment. Someone who takes my chaos and turns it into:



- Organized systems

- Clean SOPs

- Automated workflows

- Structure that scales



**Technical Requirements (Must Have):**



- **Very technical mindset** - You pick up new tools fast

- **Perfect English writing** - You may be speaking to customers every now and then on Slack, so you need perfect English.

- **Systems OCD** - You compulsively organize chaos into order

- **Extreme reliability** - Things get done without reminders



Don't need specific tool experience - but you need to be technical enough to learn any tool quickly.



If you’re the right person, and i can see you are, i’ll teach you how to use whatever we use.



**An example month**



- Week 1: maybe i tell you to "Learn cold
 systems" (you've never done it before)

- Week 2: maybe i tell you to "Build our content strategy" (completely different skill)

- Week 3: maybe i tell you to "Automate our onboarding" (another pivot)

- Week 4: maybe i tell you to "Set up tracking and analytics" (new direction)



This constant learning and pivoting is the job. If that sounds exhausting rather than exciting, don't apply.



With this job, you’ll learn more in 1 year than any corporate or VA job teaches you in 10 years. Because you’ll be learning and doing everything. You’ll build a very diverse and effective skillset.



**The Schedule:**



- **Monday-Saturday:** 9am to 6pm uk time



**Who You Need to Be:**



- **Technical** - Can learn any tool, understand systems deeply

- **OCD Organizer** - Chaos physically bothers you, you need to fix it

- **Perfect Communicator** - Your English writing is flawless and persuasive

- **Growth Minded** - You understand marketing, funnels, conversions

- **Ultra Reliable** - You never need reminders, things just get done



**Who Should NOT Apply:**



- People who get stressed by constant change

- People with just "good" English

- People who can follow systems but not build them

- People who constantly need their hand held and to be told what to do.

- People who need reminders to do recurring tasks



**Who Should Apply:**



- Technical people who pick up new tools fast.

- Obsessive organizers who hate chaos

- Resilient operators who handle stress well

- Perfect English writers who understand persuasion

- People excited by constant learning

- People who NEVER drop balls or need reminders



**The Path:**



$1,200/mo to start.



Pay rises as you become indispensable.



There is a lot of potential for growth here.



**The Bottom Line:**



This role is stressful. You'll learn new skills weekly. You'll deal with constant pivots. You'll turn chaos into order over and over again.



But if you're technical enough, organized enough, reliable enough, and resilient enough to handle this - and you become truly indispensable - you'll be paid accordingly and build a career most people can't imagine.



**To Apply:**



Send an
 with:



1. Proof of your technical abilities (what you've built/automated)

2. Example of turning chaos into an organized system

3. Why you're resilient to stress and constant change

4. Specific example of managing recurring tasks without reminders

The analyst collaborates with internal teams to ensure client information is complete, accurate, and actionable, helping improve service delivery and operational efficiency.



Roles and Responsibilities:

Create and maintain an IT book for clients, outlining the services FOS provides. Prepare site survey documents and reports to capture client infrastructure and requirements accurately. Generate client reports on tickets, labor, device health, and other key metrics. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure client information is complete, up-to-date, and actionable. Design visually appealing documents, reports, and presentations using Canva or similar design applications.



Qualification:

High school diploma required; college degree preferred. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Detail-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.



Experience:

1–2 years of experience in project management, IT, clerical work, data analytics, or a related field. Mastery of Canva and/or other design applications for creating professional reports and presentations.

title: Contract Nurse – Clinical Documentation QA & Dataset  Generation
description:
Responsibilities:



Clinical Documentation Dataset Generation:

? Use the Marlow app to record voice dictations simulating nursing documentation 

workflows. 

? Dictate responses to structured documentation prompts (e.g., UDAs) provided within the 

system. 

? Complete batches of documentation recordings within specified timelines (e.g., 100–300 

recordings over several days). 

? Follow provided instructions for consistent documentation style and structure. 

? Help generate datasets used to train and evaluate Marlow’s AI documentation system. 

Documentation QA & Review 

? Listen to recorded dictations and review the corresponding AI-generated clinical 

documentation. 

? Compare what was said in the recording with the generated structured or free-form note. 

? Identify errors or omissions in the documentation. 

? Submit corrections and suggested answers based on what was stated in the recording. 

? Follow a structured QA rubric to ensure consistent and accurate reviews. 

? Use internal review tools to efficiently listen to recordings and submit QA feedback. 

Requirements 

? Clinical background (e.g., nursing, nursing graduate, or other clinical training). 

? 1+ years of clinical experience. 

? Exceptional attention to detail. 

? Ability to follow structured QA guidelines and rubrics carefully. 

? Strong written clinical documentation skills. 

? Comfort using digital tools and web-based platforms. 

? Ability to work independently and meet deadlines. 



Preferred Qualifications:



? Experience working with U.S. healthcare documentation. 

? Background in telehealth nursing, remote clinical work, or clinical documentation. 

? Familiarity with healthcare abbreviations, shorthand, and common nursing 

documentation patterns. 

? Interest in healthcare AI and early-stage technology products.

title: Cybersecurity Specialist
description:
Cyber Security Specialist (Project-Based | Remote) 



Role Overview 

We are seeking an experienced Cyber Security Specialist to design, write, and structure a 12-week Cyber Security Job-Ready Bootcamp. This is a project-based, remote role focused on creating a practical, hands-on course that prepares learners for entry-level cyber security roles, particularly SOC Level-1 and defensive security positions. 



The program is a non-accredited, job-readiness accelerator designed to bridge the gap between academic IT studies and real-world cyber security work. 



Key Responsibilities 

Design and document a complete 12-week Cyber Security Bootcamp curriculum 

Define weekly learning objectives, lab activities, and outputs

Develop hands-on, real-world lab scenarios aligned with SOC operations and incident response 

Specify required tools, platforms, configurations, and lab environments 

Create assessment criteria aligned with performance-based evaluation (no written exams) 

Design a capstone cyber incident simulation project 

Ensure course content aligns with industry-relevant defensive cyber security skills 

Structure materials suitable for guided self-learning, live mentoring, and supervised labs 

Provide clear documentation for: 

Lab instructions 

Incident reporting templates 

Portfolio requirements 

Job-readiness outcomes 

Xavier Cyber Security short course (1) 



Required Experience & Qualifications 

Proven experience in Cyber Security / SOC / Blue Team / Incident Response 

Hands-on experience with SIEMs, endpoint security, and network analysis 

Experience designing training programs, bootcamps, or technical courses (highly preferred) 

Strong documentation and instructional design skills 

Ability to translate complex concepts into practical, hands-on learning 



Nice to Have 

SOC analyst or security certifications (e.g., Security+, CySA+, CEH, Blue Team certs) 

Teaching, mentoring, or training experience 

Experience preparing learners for entry-level cyber security roles

title: Australian Accountant
description:
Position: Accountant – Full-Time (Remote, Day Shift, Weekends Off)                       

Location: Remote (Australia) 

Experience: Minimum 4 years 

Industry Focus: Australian Accounting, Individual Tax, SMSFs 



Job Overview: 

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Accountant to join our team. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in Australian taxation, SMSFs, and accounting compliance. This is a full-time remote role with a day shift schedule and weekends off. 



Key Responsibilities: 

Finalize annual accounts and financial statements for sole traders, partnerships, companies, and trusts. 

Prepare working papers, download and reconcile reports from accounting software. 

Prepare tax returns based on finalized accounts. 

Review accounts for partner sign-off, flag discrepancies, and ensure high accuracy before final submission. 

Prepare and lodge BAS (Business Activity Statements) accurately and on time. 

Manage TPAR (Taxable Payments Annual Report) reporting and compliance. 

Apply knowledge of Division 7A loan agreements, minimum yearly repayments, and compliance requirements. 

Calculate and prepare Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT) returns. 

Maintain strong proficiency in Xero and QuickBooks; experience with LodgeiT is a plus. 



Requirements: 

Minimum 4 years of experience in accounting for Australian clients. 

Strong experience with individual tax preparation and SMSFs. 

Thorough knowledge of Australian tax legislation, compliance, and reporting requirements. 

Excellent attention to detail and accuracy. 

Ability to work independently in a remote environment. 



Benefits: 

Full-time, remote position 

Day shift with weekends off 

Opportunity to work with Australian clients and gain exposure to diverse accounting practices

I’m mainly looking for someone with a few specific skills. First, short form video editing. I have concert footage, rehearsal clips, and studio 
nts that I’d like turned into engaging clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. Experience with tools like CapCut or Adobe Premiere is a big plus.

title: Aging Claims Resolution Specialist
description:
Job Overview



MODPractice is seeking a focused, detail-driven Aging Claims Specialist to support dental practices by resolving outstanding insurance claims and reducing accounts receivable. This role is dedicated to insurance AR follow-up and requires a strong understanding of payer behavior, documentation requirements, and effective escalation strategies.



The ideal candidate is persistent, organized, and comfortable working with aging claims daily while maintaining clear documentation and measurable productivity. This position plays a direct role in improving cash flow for our client practices and supporting sustainable revenue-cycle systems.



Key Responsibilities



-Resolving outstanding dental insurance claims over 30 days from oldest to newest to drive timely payment.

-Research claim delays, denials, and underpayments; identify root causes and take corrective action.

-Submit corrected claims, appeals, narratives, and supporting documentation as required by payers.

-Communicate directly with insurance carriers via phone and online portals to obtain claim status and resolution.

-Accurately post insurance payments and adjustments once claims are resolved.

-Maintain thorough, organized documentation of all claim activity and follow-up efforts.

-Identify payer trends, recurring denial issues, or process breakdowns and escalate appropriately.

-Collaborate with internal MODPractice team members and client offices to correct eligibility, coding, or documentation issues impacting claims.

-Ensure compliance with HIPAA, payer guidelines, and MODPractice revenue-cycle standards.

-Productivity Expectations & KPIs



This role is performance-driven with clearly defined productivity benchmarks:

Minimum Productivity Requirement: 30 insurance claims worked per day (baseline expectation)



Tiered Performance Bonuses:

-Tier 1 Bonus: 45 claims worked per day

-Tier 2 Bonus: 60 claims worked per day



“Claims worked” includes documented follow-up activity such as calls, portal actions, corrections, appeals, and payment posting where applicable.



Qualifications

-Experience: Minimum of 2 years of hands-on dental insurance aging and accounts receivable experience.

-Strong understanding of dental insurance workflows, EOB interpretation, and payer follow-up strategies.

-Proficiency with dental practice management software (Dentrix, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, etc.) and insurance portals.

-Working knowledge of CDT codes and common denial reasons.

-Highly organized with excellent attention to detail and time management skills.

-Comfortable working independently in a structured, KPI-driven environment.

-Clear, professional communication skills—both written and verbal.

-Ability to maintain consistency, accuracy, and productivity in a fast-paced environment.



Why MODPractice

MODPractice partners with dental practices to create revenue-cycle systems that are efficient, ethical, and sustainable. We value clarity, accountability, and professionalism—while providing the tools and support needed for team members to succeed.



How to Apply

Please complete the virtual interview at:

app.xinterview.ai/direct_invite/816ceba8-466a-4f9e-a792-a0b693107f7d



We will follow up with an interview request after reviewing your application.

title: Virtual Scheduler & Office Administrator (Home Care Agency – Full Time Remote)
description:
We Are Looking for a Rockstar Scheduler & Office Admin



We are a growing Home Care Agency looking for a high-performing Virtual Scheduler & Office Administrator to join our team full-time. This role is critical to our operations and requires someone who is organized, tech-savvy, compassionate, and excellent at communication.



You will be responsible for managing caregiver schedules, coordinating with clients and staff, and ensuring that all care shifts are filled quickly and efficiently.



Our team is close-knit, supportive, and focused on delivering exceptional care to our clients and their families. We build relationships with our caregivers and clients from the very first phone call — and you will play a key role in that experience.

If you are detail-oriented, fast, reliable, and love working with people, we would love to hear from you.

________________________________________

IMPORTANT – PLEASE READ BEFORE APPLYING

Please apply ONLY if you meet the following criteria:

• You have excellent English communication skills (clear, professional, neutral accent)

• You are comfortable speaking on the phone with clients and caregivers daily

• You can commit to full-time work (40 hours per week)

• You have reliable (Wired with backup) high-speed internet and a professional home office setup

If you cannot meet these requirements, please do not apply.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

Scheduling & Operations

• Manage daily caregiver scheduling and shift coordination

• Match the right caregiver to the right client based on needs, skills, and location

• Maintain an accurate caregiver availability chart

• Ensure all shifts are filled quickly and efficiently

• Monitor caregiver overtime and travel time

Client & Caregiver Communication

• Answer incoming calls from clients and families professionally

• Respond to scheduling requests, service inquiries, and questions

• Communicate regularly with caregivers to confirm schedules and shift coverage

• Handle last-minute scheduling changes and emergencies



Client Intake & Administration

• Process new client service requests and intake information

• Enter and update client data in the agency's software system

• Prepare reports, correspondence, and documentation

• Coordinate with the Operations Director and team members

Care Coordination

• Provide caregivers with accurate directions and client care instructions

• Follow up with caregivers regarding scheduling issues

• Communicate with clients, families, and referral partners when needed

________________________________________

Requirements

Experience

• 2–3 years of scheduling or administrative experience

• Experience working as a Virtual Assistant or Remote Administrator preferred

• Experience in home care, healthcare, or staffing coordination is a strong plus

Skills

• Excellent English communication skills (spoken and written)

• Strong customer service and people skills

• Excellent organization and time management

• Strong problem-solving ability

• Detail-oriented and highly dependable

• Able to work independently and as part of a team

Technical Skills

Must be comfortable with:

• Microsoft Excel, Word, and Google Workspace

• Scheduling software and digital communication tools

•
, instant messaging, and phone-based support

• Basic document preparation and data entry

________________________________________

Technical Requirements (Mandatory)

• Two monitors / dual screen setup

• Reliable high-speed wired internet (minimum 25 Mbps)

• Noise-cancelling headset with microphone

• Quiet, professional home office environment

________________________________________

About Our Company

A few things you should know about working with us:

• We are a Home Care Agency providing private-duty services for clients of all ages

• We move fast and value efficiency and accountability

• We treat our team members with respect, loyalty, and fairness

• If the business grows, our team benefits from that growth

We are looking for someone who wants to grow with us long-term — not just short-term work.

________________________________________

Compensation & Benefits

• Weekly or bi-weekly pay

• Performance-based bonuses

• Pay increases based on performance and contribution

• Long-term growth opportunity

________________________________________

How to Apply

If you believe you are a great fit for this role, please follow the steps below:

1. Use this format for the subject line:

Virtual Scheduler – Your Name

2. In the first line of your application, please write:

“I am your Rockstar Scheduler.”

3. Record a short video introducing yourself, explaining:

• Who you are

• Your scheduling/customer service experience

• Why you would be a great fit for this role

4.  Attach your resume

5.  Include a link to your OnlineJobs.ph profile 

Applications that do not follow these outlined steps may not be considered.

________________________________________

Final Note

This is a long-term position. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with our company for years — not weeks.

If you are organized, proactive, compassionate, and great with people, we would love to meet you.

title: Virtual Assistant / Recruiter to Source Developers
description:
I'm looking for a reliable long-term VA to help me find the right person as a consultant.



The developer I need isn't your typical heads-down coder. They'll spend a lot of their time talking directly with clients on video calls. Finding someone who is both technically solid and genuinely great to talk to is harder than it sounds, and that's exactly what I need your help with.



Your job will be to source candidates and get each one on a video call before you bring them to me. You'll be the first filter in the process.



The developer needs at least 5 years of full-stack experience. Native-level spoken English is a hard requirement, and you'll be the one verifying it during the call.



When you bring a candidate to me, please include a short note about your impression from the call (their communication style, confidence on video, and whether you'd personally recommend them).



I prefer a VA who has experience as a recruiter. However, experience is not everything. Someone who is talented at sourcing people, sincere, honest, and willing to do the work is also very welcome.



If this works well, I'm looking for a long-term collaboration with the right VA.

title: Bookkeeper
description:
Senior Bookkeeper.

Retainer Contact | Remote in LATAM.



About Your Startup Operations.

Your Startup Operations is a boutique agency providing high-level operational services to small businesses. We work primarily with business owners in the United States. Our vision is to empower small business owners by delivering "white glove" service that maintains flexibility, care, and affordability.



Perks & benefits.

Effective immediately:



* 7 paid company holidays (major U.S. holidays listed in original JD).

* Regularly paid company social events and game nights.

* Bi-monthly team training.



After 3 months:



* 1 local holiday choice per year.

* $100/year professional development allowance.

* One-time $50 workspace reimbursement.

* 10 days PTO / 3 sick days per year.



After 1 year:



* Healthcare reimbursement up to $50/month (eligible expenses).

* An additional one-time $50 workspace reimbursement.

* Possible year-end bonus (company discretion).



Who You Are.



* Proactive and resourceful: anticipate needs and solve problems before they become issues.

* Highly organized: establish clear day-to-day routines and keep complex workflows running smoothly.

* A strong communicator: articulate ideas clearly and professionally in both written and verbal English.

* Detail-oriented: care about accuracy.



Key Responsibilities.



* Manage financial records: execute full-cycle bookkeeping in QuickBooks Online for a roster of clients, ensuring US GAAP compliance.

* Reconciliation and reporting: perform monthly bank and credit card reconciliations and generate accurate financial reports (P&L, Balance Sheet).

* AP/AR management: oversee invoicing and bill payments using tools like Bill.com and Stripe.

* Process optimization: continuously look for ways to streamline financial workflows using tools like Hubdoc and 17hats.

* Client support: act as a reliable financial resource, maintaining professional correspondence with clients regarding their accounts.



Requirements.

The essentials:



* Experience: 5+ years of dedicated bookkeeping experience.

* International exposure: 2+ years of experience working with American, Canadian, Australian, or U.K. clients/managers.

* Technical mastery: 2+ years of hands-on experience with QuickBooks Online is mandatory.

* Experience in non-profit bookkeeping: done books for non-profit organizations.

* Adaptability: ability to quickly learn new tools and find efficient solutions to day-to-day issues.

* Availability: flexibility to take calls during PST to EST business hours.



Preferred skills.



* Experience with US GAAP standards.

* Familiarity with project management tools (Asana, Trello, or ClickUp).

* Proficiency with financial ecosystem tools: Stripe, Hubdoc, 17hats, Bill.com, Gusto, and Wise.

* Knowledge of IOLTA (Trust Accounting) is a significant advantage.

title: Video Editor for Fast-Growing DTC Wellness Brand (VSLs, UGC, & AI Tools)
description:
We’re a fast-growing European direct-to-consumer (DTC) wellness brand helping people break bad habits and live healthier, more mindful lives.



Our flagship product is a flavored air device — a clean, modern, and satisfying alternative to old routines — and we’re scaling fast across global markets.



We’re now building one of Europe’s most effective creative marketing teams and are searching for a Video Editor who can turn ideas into scroll-stopping, high-converting VSLs and Meta ad creatives.



If you love storytelling, psychology, and data — and you’re obsessed with crafting videos that sell — this might be your next big opportunity.





What You’ll Do

- Edit short-form VSLs (2–3 mins) and UGC-style ads for
, Instagram & TikTok

- Turn testimonials, product shots, and voiceovers into conversion-focused video assets

- Produce multiple ad variations for A/B testing and performance optimization

- Source high-quality UGC, B-roll, and sound effects from TikTok, YouTube, or stock sites

- Use AI tools (avatars, voiceovers, B-roll generation, etc.) to boost creative output

- Work closely with our creative strategist and performance team to test and iterate fast

- Keep up with trends on Reels, TikTok & YouTube Shorts in the wellness/lifestyle niche



What We’re Looking For

- 1–2+ years of proven experience editing VSLs or performance ads for DTC/eCom brands

- Solid understanding of direct-response marketing & buyer psychology

- Skilled in CapCut Pro, Premiere Pro, or similar tools

- Experience using or open to learning AI video tools (HeyGen, VEO 3, ElevenLabs, etc.)

- Ability to source and curate UGC or short-form content independently

- Strong communicator — reliable, organized, and proactive

- Excellent English (written & spoken)



This Role Isn’t for You If…

- You care only about aesthetics, not results and performance data

- You can’t handle tight deadlines or fast feedback loops

- You’ve never edited Meta VSL ads or don’t understand DTC ad structure

- You need constant supervision or hand-holding

- You’re not available 10–20 hours per week on a consistent basis



Why You’ll Love Working With Us

- Be part of a high-performance, remote-first team that values speed & creativity

- Direct access to experienced creative strategists and media buyers

- Real ownership — your edits directly drive revenue & growth

- Freedom to experiment with AI tools, trends & creative strategies

- Long-term growth opportunity with a fast-scaling European brand

- Competitive pay + performance-based bonuses for top results



Ready to Apply



- We only review applications submitted via Typeform.

Please copy & paste this link into your browser (remove spaces):

form .typeform. com/to/ dO9pZYvu



It takes under 8 minutes, and we’ll respond within 3–5 business days.

title: Used Car Researcher Porsche 911 Deal Finder VIN Required Clean Title Verification
description:
We are hiring a detail oriented online researcher to locate underpriced Porsche vehicles listed for sale in the United States.



Your job is to search multiple car listing websites and identify Porsche 911 vehicles that appear to be priced below market value.



You will collect detailed information about each vehicle, obtain the VIN number, confirm the vehicle has a clean title, estimate market values using the Carfax vehicle value tool, and enter all information into a spreadsheet.



For listings that appear to be strong opportunities, you will also contact the seller to confirm the vehicle is still available and ask if the seller has flexibility on price.



This role requires careful research, attention to detail, and the ability to follow instructions precisely.



We are not looking for someone who simply copies listings. We want someone who can identify potential deals and gather all necessary information.



?



Target Vehicles



Primary focus



Porsche 911



Model years

2013 through 2020



Mileage requirement

Under 70,000 miles



Title requirement

Clean title only



Cars with salvage titles, rebuilt titles, flood damage, or unclear ownership should NOT be included.



?



Websites You Must Search



You must search the following websites every day.



Primary search tool



AutoTempest



AutoTempest combines listings from many major websites and should be used to locate listings quickly across multiple platforms.



Major marketplaces



Autotrader

Cars.com


 Marketplace

Craigslist



Enthusiast marketplaces



Rennlist classifieds

Porsche Club of America classifieds



Auction marketplaces



Bring a Trailer

Cars and Bids



You may also search other car listing websites if you believe they contain relevant listings.



?



Important Regions to Focus On



When searching
 Marketplace and Craigslist, focus on major metropolitan areas where Porsche ownership is common and where vehicles are frequently sold privately.



Important regions include



California



Los Angeles

Orange County

San Diego

San Francisco Bay Area

San Jose

Sacramento



Texas



Dallas

Austin

Houston



Florida



Miami

Fort Lauderdale

Palm Beach

Tampa

Orlando



Arizona



Scottsdale

Phoenix



Nevada



Las Vegas



Colorado



Denver

Boulder



Washington



Seattle

Bellevue



Oregon



Portland



Illinois



Chicago



New York



New York City

Long Island

Westchester County



Massachusetts



Boston



Georgia



Atlanta



North Carolina



Charlotte

Raleigh



These areas often have a high number of Porsche vehicles and more private sellers.



?



Determining Market Value Using Carfax



For every vehicle you find, you must determine estimated market values using the Carfax Value Tool.



Use this page







Enter the VIN number and mileage to determine the estimated values.



You must record the following values from Carfax



Retail value

Private party value

Trade in value



These values must be included in the spreadsheet along with the listing price.



?



How to Identify a Good Deal



You will compare the listing price with other similar vehicles and with the Carfax estimated values.



If the listing price is significantly lower than the Carfax private party value or retail value, the listing may represent a good opportunity.



Listings priced at least seven thousand dollars below comparable vehicles or Carfax value estimates should be flagged.



Signs that a seller may be motivated include



price reductions

listing has been active for more than thirty days

seller says must sell

seller is moving or relocating

estate sale

poor photos or incomplete description



Listings with these signals should be flagged.



?



Required Data For Each Listing



Every vehicle you submit must include the following information.



Listing link



Website source



Year



Model



Mileage



Price



Location city and state



Seller phone number or contact method



VIN number



Confirmation that title is clean



Carfax retail value



Carfax private party value



Carfax trade in value



Estimated difference between listing price and private party value



Notes explaining why you believe the vehicle may be a good opportunity.



?



VIN Requirement



Every vehicle must include the VIN.



If the VIN is not listed in the advertisement, you must contact the seller and politely ask for it.



If the seller refuses to provide the VIN, do not include the vehicle in the spreadsheet.



?



Title Verification Requirement



You must confirm the vehicle has a clean title.



You can verify this by



reading the advertisement carefully

asking the seller directly



If the seller confirms the title is clean, record that confirmation in the spreadsheet notes.



Vehicles with salvage titles, rebuilt titles, or unknown title status should not be included.



?



Contacting the Seller



For vehicles that appear to be good opportunities, you will contact the seller through the listing platform.



Your goals are to confirm



the car is still available



the VIN number if it is missing



the title is clean



whether the seller has flexibility on price.



You may use a message similar to the following



Hello. I saw your Porsche listing and wanted to ask if the vehicle is still available. I also wanted to confirm the VIN number and that the title is clean. Are you flexible on the price if a buyer can move quickly?



If the seller responds, record their response in the spreadsheet.



?



Spreadsheet Format



All vehicles must be entered into a shared Google Sheet.



Columns will include



Listing link

Website source

Year

Model

Mileage

Price

Carfax retail value

Carfax private party value

Carfax trade in value

Difference between listing price and private party value

VIN

Seller name

Seller phone or contact method

City and state

Clean title confirmed yes or no

Seller response

Notes



?



Daily Work Expectations



You will search for vehicles approximately three to four hours per day.



You should submit between ten and twenty vehicles per day that appear to be priced below market value.



Quality is more important than quantity.



?



Important Rules



Do not include vehicles with salvage titles or rebuilt titles.



Do not include vehicles without a VIN.



Do not include vehicles where title status cannot be confirmed.



Focus primarily on private sellers rather than dealerships.



Only include vehicles that appear to be significantly underpriced.



?



Skills Required



Good written English



Strong attention to detail



Ability to research vehicle pricing



Comfort using spreadsheets



Ability to communicate politely with sellers online.



?



Compensation



Hourly pay.



Reliable workers may receive additional hours and long term work.



High quality work will lead to increased hours.

title: Course Developer (Virtual Assistant) – Part-Time | Remote
description:
Role: Course Developer (VA)

Hours: Approximately 20 hours per week

Schedule: Must be available to work EST hours

Location: Remote

Rate: $3–$4 USD per hour

________________________________________



About Riskophia



At Riskophia, we’re building a people-first compliance ecosystem that helps organizations in regulated industries turn training into real behavior change.



As we expand our learning platform through Learnophia, we’re looking for a Course Developer VA who can transform content into engaging, visually compelling learning experiences.



________________________________________



What you’ll be doing



• Translate content into clear instructional storyboards

• Develop interactive eLearning modules using Articulate Storyline or other instructional design tools

• Structure content for engagement, clarity, and strong learner flow

• Apply instructional design principles (adult learning, scenario-based learning, microlearning)

• Upload and manage courses within an LMS

• Collaborate with our team to refine content and improve learner experience

• Support updates and enhancements to existing courses

• We’re looking for someone who:

title: Video Editor / Sound Designer for Cinematic YouTube Channel
description:
Video Editor / Sound Designer for Cinematic YouTube Channel

Japanese culture, aesthetics, art, and craft



I am looking for a creative collaborator to support my personal YouTube channel on an ongoing basis.



My channel focuses on essay-style videos and vlogs centred on Japanese culture, aesthetics, art, craft, and visual storytelling. It is a personal creative project with a strong point of view, and the overall tone is cinematic, thoughtful, and mood-driven.



Because of that, I am especially looking for someone who is genuinely strong at cinematic editing tone — particularly in the areas of music selection, sound design, colour grading, rhythm, and emotional atmosphere.



What I am mainly looking for at this stage is someone who can elevate the final creative layer of each video — particularly through sound design, music selection, colour grading, and final polish.



In most cases, I will already have the voiceover recorded, the rough edit largely assembled, and the footage placed into the timeline in a rough structure.



What I need is someone who can take that near-finished project and bring it to a more polished, immersive, and publish-ready level.



Main responsibilities



Your main role would be to support the project through:

 • Sound design

 • selecting music that truly fits the mood, pacing, and emotional tone of the video

 • sourcing tracks from Artlist

 • adding and balancing sound effects where needed

 • shaping the audio so the overall viewing experience feels immersive and polished

 • Colour grading

 • grading or adjusting footage shot in a mix of F-Log and Rec.709

 • matching the visual tone across clips

 • helping maintain a consistent look that fits the mood of my previous videos

 • Final polish

 • bringing the edit to a clean, refined, publish-ready state

 • making sure the final piece feels cohesive in tone, rhythm, and finish



Potential future scope



For now, the role is focused mainly on sound, grading, and final polish.



However, if the collaboration goes well, there is definitely potential for the role to expand in future. This could include more complete editing support, such as:

 • shaping scenes more actively to voiceover

 • building out edits from footage pools

 • refining transitions and visual flow

 • contributing more directly to structure, pacing, and retention



So while the immediate need is not full editing, I would ideally like to find someone who could grow with the project over time.



What matters most to me



The single most important thing for me is musical taste and sound sensibility.



I am looking for someone who has a real instinct for music — someone who can choose tracks with sensitivity, bring unexpected but fitting ideas, and strengthen the emotional layer of a scene in a thoughtful way.



I also care a lot about:

 • a strong sense of cinematic tone

 • thoughtful sound design

 • tasteful colour grading

 • sensitivity to pacing and emotional build

 • the ability to make the final video feel intentional and immersive



YouTube awareness



I would also really value someone who has a good instinct for pacing, viewer engagement, and retention, and who can make thoughtful suggestions where needed.



Nice to have



The following are not essential, but are definitely a plus:

 • interest in Japanese culture, aesthetics, art, craft, or design

 • experience with essay-style or cinematic YouTube content

 • the confidence to make thoughtful creative suggestions when appropriate



Workflow



I work in DaVinci Resolve, and I would like the collaboration to happen within DaVinci Resolve / Resolve Cloud as much as possible.



Volume and collaboration style



I am currently aiming to publish around 2–3 videos per month, usually in the 10–20 minute range.



Since this is an ongoing personal creative project, I am ideally looking for someone who is open to a long-term collaboration, rather than a one-off freelancer for a single video.



I believe the work becomes much stronger once someone begins to understand the channel’s style, taste, and emotional tone over time.



Communication



Clear, prompt, and reliable communication is very important to me.



Payment



At the beginning, I would prefer to work on an hourly basis for the first 1–2 videos, simply so we can both understand the actual time involved and establish a good workflow together.



After that, if the collaboration feels like a good fit, I would ideally like to move to a fixed per-video rate.



To apply



Please send:

 • your portfolio

 • links to videos you personally edited

 • examples of work that show strong sound design, music selection, colour grading, and cinematic tone

 • a short note explaining which parts of each video were specifically your responsibility

 • your hourly rate

 • whether you would be open to an ongoing collaboration

 • whether you are comfortable working in DaVinci Resolve / Resolve Cloud

 • whether you hold an Artlist licence

 • your general turnaround time for this kind of work



If it feels like a good fit, I may also ask for a small paid test task before making a final decision.



This is a project I care deeply about, so I am looking for someone with real taste, sensitivity, and passion for video editing — someone who would enjoy contributing to the long-term growth of a thoughtful creative project.



You can get a sense of the channel’s tone and style here:

YouTube:

title: Performance Marketing Solutions Architect (Meta & Google Ads)
description:
Performance Marketing Solutions Architect



We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Solutions Architect who deeply understands campaign architecture, performance strategy, and the latest AI-driven optimisation frameworks across Meta and Google Ads.



This role is not just about pushing buttons. It’s about designing the right campaign strategy from day one.



You’ll work closely with our Director and Campaign Performance Manager, helping design campaign structures that align with client goals across lead generation, e-commerce, and high-ROAS performance campaigns.



If you can look at a client’s business model and immediately know how the Meta and Google campaign architecture should be built, we want to hear from you.



What You'll Be Responsible For



Designing Meta Ads and Google Ads campaign architecture and strategy



Translating client goals into scalable campaign structures



Reviewing client meeting notes and strategic briefs provided by the Director



Building campaign strategies based on client goals, KPIs, and business models



Structuring campaigns aligned with Meta’s Andromeda AI optimisation framework



Structuring Google Ads campaigns across Search, Performance Max, and Shopping



Advising on creative strategy and testing frameworks



Working with our Campaign Performance Manager on performance optimisation



Reviewing existing accounts and identifying structural improvements



Supporting implementation where needed



Developing cross-platform strategies across Meta and Google



How You'll Work With Our Team



You’ll collaborate closely with our Director and Campaign Performance Manager to turn client goals into high-performing paid media strategies.



After client meetings, you’ll receive meeting notes outlining the client’s objectives, business model, KPIs, and expectations. From there, your role is to design the campaign strategy and architecture that best achieves those outcomes.



You’ll act as the performance marketing architect, ensuring campaigns are structured correctly from the beginning so they can scale efficiently across both Meta and Google ecosystems.



What We're Looking For



5+ years experience running Meta Ads and/or Google Ads campaigns



Strong experience across e-commerce and lead generation



Proven ability to deliver ROAS-driven performance campaigns



Deep understanding of Meta campaign architecture and scaling strategies



Strong knowledge of Meta’s Andromeda update and AI-driven optimisation



Strong understanding of Google Ads campaign structure and automation frameworks



Strong creative strategy instincts (you understand what makes ads convert)



Ability to analyse an account quickly and recommend clear structural improvements



Experience collaborating with performance teams and strategists



What Makes This Role Different



This is not just a media buyer role.



We’re looking for someone who thinks like a performance strategist and solutions architect — someone who can design the right campaign system for the right business across both Meta and Google ecosystems.



You’ll help shape how we structure campaigns across our client portfolio and work alongside a team focused on serious performance marketing.



Ideal Candidate



You might be:



A senior Meta or Google Ads strategist



A performance marketing lead at an agency



A paid media architect responsible for campaign strategy



Someone who loves analysing accounts and building better campaign structures

title: Amazon Catalog Manager - EU Marketplace
description:
About the Company

We are a leading Dutch e-commerce brand in the Home Improvement & Kitchen niche. After dominating our local market, we expanded into Germany (DE) at the start of 2026. We currently manage over 100+ ASINs across 50+ product families and are looking for a meticulous Catalog Manager to maintain and optimize our expanding inventory.



The Role & Workflow

You will be working directly alongside our Amazon Account Manager. We operate with a structured Task List system. Your primary responsibility is the flawless execution of tasks assigned by the Account Manager, ensuring deadlines are met and updates are logged.



High-level communication and status tracking are mandatory. We need someone who doesn't just "do the work," but also makes the results of their optimizations visible and measurable for the team.



Requirements

Experience: 3+ years of dedicated Amazon Catalog/Listing Management.



Collaboration: Proven experience working under an Account Manager or Project Manager using task management tools (e.g., Asana, ClickUp, Trello, or Monday).



Communication: Exceptional English communication skills. You must provide regular status updates and be proactive in reporting roadblocks.



Niche Expertise: Direct experience in Home Improvement or Kitchen categories.



Technical Skills: Expert in creating complex Product Families (Parent/Child variations) and Flat File management.



Fulfillment: Experience switching/managing listings between FBA and FBM seamlessly.



Localization: Ability to manage/optimize listings in German and Dutch (using localization tools and AI) to ensure high-quality SEO.



Key Responsibilities

Execution: Perform daily tasks from the Task List provided by the Account Manager.



Status Tracking: Maintain a clear log of "Before vs. After" for all listing optimizations to show impact on conversion and ranking.



Catalog Health: Organize 100+ ASINs into logical product families and resolve suppressed listings or stranded inventory immediately.



Fulfillment Management: Manage the transition of listings from FBA to FBM based on stock levels and strategy.



Reporting: Provide a brief daily or weekly update on the status of pending tasks and completed optimizations.



What We Offer

A structured work environment with clear instructions.



Long-term partnership with a professional, growing European brand.



Tools and support needed to perform at a high level.



How to Apply

To ensure you’ve read this post thoroughly, please start your application with the word "HAMMER" and include the following:



A summary of your experience managing catalogs in the Home Improvement/Kitchen niche.



Which task management tools (Asana/ClickUp etc.) are you most comfortable with?



How do you track and report the results of a listing optimization (e.g., how do you show us that your change actually worked)?



Your desired monthly rate or hourly expectation.

 Profile Management

 my

 selected 
 posts for

•

To Apply

3. A short description of your experience supporting consultants, coaches, or IT professionals

If you enjoy creating thoughtful LinkedIn content, optimising personal brands, editing simple video content, engaging in professional conversations, and supporting a growing coaching business, I’d love to hear from you.

title: Customer Success & Operations Manager - $1500/mo
description:
Location: Remote (Work from Home)

Employment Type: Full-Time

Salary: $1,500/month USD



About the Role

--

This is not a standard support job.

You will be the sole owner of everything client-facing and a key operational support to the founders at a fast-growing UK-based SaaS company. The founders currently handle all of this — and we're handing it off completely to the right person. Every support query, every onboarding call, every payment issue, every client
, every administrative task — that's you. Full ownership from day one.



How the role actually works

--

This is not a 9-to-5 where you clock in and clock out. On an average day you'll receive around 30 messages from clients. Most are quick to resolve. A handful will need more digging — you'll triage these, gather all the relevant information, and pass them to the engineering team with a clear summary so they can resolve it fast. Once a day or so you'll jump on a short call with a client to walk them through something, screen share, or onboard them.



There will be stretches where nothing comes in for hours. Then stretches where five things need attention at once. In exchange for a salary that is well above market rate, what we ask is simple — while you're awake, clients get replied to. You don't need to be glued to your desk all day. But you should never be more than an hour away from responding during your waking hours. That reliability is the entire job. We're looking for someone who is fine with this. If you want a simple 8 hours a day 9-5 clock in, clock out, then this job is not for you.



On weekends, a once-a-day check-in to reply to any outstanding messages is all we ask.



What you'll own

--

- All inbound client communication — support queries,
s, messages

- Onboarding new customers via video call — screen sharing, walkthroughs, getting them set up

- Triaging technical issues — gathering all relevant details and passing them to the engineering team with a clear, organised summary

- Proactively following up with clients to make sure they're getting results

- Recovering failed payments and chasing unpaid invoices

, or SaaS tools is a strong bonus

title: Part-Time CapCut/TikTok Video Editor
description:
Hiring for a part-time video editor with potential for more. You will make short TikTok videos using CapCut or software of your choice. The video style is fun montages with DJ music/remixes, fast pace and high energy. 



What you will do:

* Take clips of videos and structure them into a montage

* Identify what clips serve as the best hook to catch attention of the viewer

* Identify the different parts of the song (intro, come up, beat drop)

* Identify best clips to use for the video (reactions, cool stunts, scenery, funny moments)

* Match clips to lyrics, vibe, and/or vibe of remix/audios

* Understand how to use clips to catch viewers attention with hooks, and to hold attention with anticipation, tension, and release. 

* Use color grading (HSL, adjust settings, curves) to make clips look uniform to one another, and naturally enhance visuals

* Subtly use zoom, keyframe, and speed features to make clips engaging, make clips look uniform to one another, and enhance actions performed in videos

* Effects will NOT be used most of the time, if so, very minimally in a way that is not overly noticeable



Every video must follow this structure:

* Hook (0-2 seconds) - show the most surprising or exciting clip first. This stops people from scrolling.

* Tension/Come Up (2-12 seconds) - show more clips, match the vibe, lyrics, and beat of the music. 

* Release/Beat Drop - highest energy clips, pay off of the video



What makes a good hook? The first 2 seconds must make the viewer feel something. Here are the feelings that work best:

* Surprise/Shock- they see something they did not expect

* Fear of missing out (FOMO) - they feel like they are missing out on something fun

* Curiosity - they think "wait, what is that?" and keep watching

* Relatability - they think "that is me" or "I want that life"



Please review a few examples of my content to ensure you are familiar and comfortable with the editing style:



Requirements:

* You have edited viral TikTok or Instagram Reels videos before.

* You can match video cuts to music beats and/or lyrics

* You can tell which clips have emotion and which ones are boring.

* You speak strong english, and understand lyrics of the songs.

* You pay close attention to details.

* You can take feedback and make changes fast.

* You have a strong internet connection.

* You can work 5-6 days per week.

* You can create & send at least 1 video per day

* You can receive payments through Wise.

* You are in the Philippines or a similar time zone.



Pay:

* $300/month ($10 per video for 30 videos) + bonuses for high performing videos. 2-3 hours a day MAX, with potential full time work in the future with a salary increase.



How to Apply:

* Please complete the recreate assignment below so I can see your skills (1 hour max). 

* Watch reference videos to understand how the final result should look. 

* However, make hooks more dramatic, engaging, and surprising than reference videos. 

* Extra: If you have created projects similar to this style in the past, please send them as well for consideration.



Raw Footage: 

Sample Audio & Reference Video: 

Upload Your Video: 



Please complete the following steps:

1. Download the raw footage and import into your video editing software

2. Import the sample audio, set bookmarks or use beats feature to identify where each clip will start and end

3. Select best clips to use for hook, come up, and beat drop

4. Adjust shadows, saturation, brilliance, HSL to match videos visually, and enhance colors naturally

5. Use zoom, keyframes, and speed minimally and naturally to retain attention and enhance actions in the clips

6. Upload your sample to the Google Drive folder OR send us a link to your sample video. 



We look forward to working with you!

title: Remote | Part-Time | Long-Term Collaboration
description:
**Remote | Part-Time | Long-Term Collaboration**



**Position: Google Ads Specialist**



We are seeking an experienced Google Ads Specialist to support the planning, management, and optimization of paid advertising campaigns. The ideal candidate understands how to use data, testing, and structured campaign strategies to improve performance and achieve consistent results.



This position is best suited for professionals who already have hands-on experience managing Google Ads accounts and are comfortable working independently in a remote environment.



**Key Responsibilities**



* Plan, build, and manage Google Ads campaigns including Search, Display, and YouTube.

* Organize campaign structures that align with clear marketing and conversion goals.

* Monitor campaign performance and optimize based on metrics such as CTR, CPC, CPA, and ROAS.

* Perform keyword research and review competitor strategies to identify opportunities.

* Test variations of ad copy, targeting, and bidding strategies to improve results.

* Set up and maintain conversion tracking using Google Analytics and Google Tag Manager.

* Prepare periodic performance reports with insights and recommendations.

* Coordinate with the team to ensure campaigns support overall business objectives.



**Requirements**



* Minimum of 3 years of practical experience managing Google Ads campaigns.

* Strong understanding of campaign setup, bidding strategies, and optimization methods.

* Experience with conversion tracking, Google Analytics (GA4), and Google Tag Manager.

* Ability to interpret data and make performance-driven adjustments.

* Clear communication skills and the ability to work in a remote setting.

* Stable internet connection and a reliable workspace.



**Preferred Qualifications**



* Experience managing higher monthly ad budgets.

* Previous experience working with international clients.

* Understanding of landing page performance and conversion-focused campaigns.

* Experience with e-commerce or lead generation projects.



**Compensation**



* Pay will be based on experience and skills.

* Opportunity for ongoing collaboration on future projects.

* Performance incentives may be available.



**Application**

If you are interested in this opportunity, please send your CV and a short summary of your Google Ads experience to:





Selected candidates will be contacted for the next steps.

title: Roofing Lead Intake Specialist
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking a responsive and professional Roofing Lead Intake Specialist to manage inbound calls and capture new customer inquiries for a growing roofing company.

In this role, you will be responsible for answering incoming calls promptly, assisting potential customers, and ensuring all lead information is accurately recorded in the system. Experience in the roofing industry is required.

This position will begin as part-time, with the opportunity to transition into full-time.



Key Responsibilities

• Answer incoming calls from homeowners and potential customers.

• Respond to inbound leads within 45–60 seconds whenever possible.

• Keep the dialer system active and monitored during scheduled work hours.

• Accurately enter and update lead information in the system.

• Provide clear, friendly, and professional communication with callers.

• Schedule appointments or route calls to the appropriate team member when necessary.

• Ensure all inbound leads are properly documented and tracked.



Qualifications

• Experience in the roofing industry.

• Experience using Open Dental.

• Previous experience in customer service, call handling, or lead intake preferred.

• Strong phone presence and communication skills.

• Detail-oriented with the ability to accurately capture lead information.

• Reliable internet connection and ability to respond quickly to incoming calls.

title: Creative Strategist - E-Commerce Brands (Spanish & International Markets)
description:
We're building a high-performance creative team for our e-commerce brands and need a Creative Strategist who can own the entire creative strategy process. You'll work hand-in-hand with our video editor to create a seamless, automated content production system that drives conversions.



This is NOT a freelance or project-based position. We're looking for a long-term team member who wants to grow with us.



YOUR MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES:



Creative Strategy & Direction:

- Develop comprehensive creative strategies based on market research, customer insights, and competitor analysis.

- Write high-converting ad scripts and copy that speak directly to customer pain points and objections

- Research and identify customer avatars, their desires, fears, and buying triggers

- Conduct ongoing market research to stay ahead of trends in our niches

- Analyze creative performance data and iterate strategies accordingly

*Proven internal AI workflows for all of these tasks are provided in addition to your skills.*



Creator & UGC Management:

- Source, vet, and manage content creators and UGC creators for our brands

- Provide clear briefs, scripts, and creative direction to creators



Content Planning & Production Oversight:

- Create detailed creative briefs for our video editor based on strategic insights

- Collaborate with our editor to streamline the production workflow



Primary focus: Spanish market

Secondary focus: US and other EU markets for new brand testing

Adapt strategies and messaging for different cultural contexts and markets



WHO YOU ARE:



Required Skills & Experience:



- Copywriting skills in Spanish, English and other languages, also with the help of AI

- Strong English communication skills for internal team communication

- Proven track record of creating ad creatives that converted for other DTC brands

- Deep understanding of direct response marketing principles and scriptwriting process

- Experience sourcing and managing content creators/UGC creators 

- Up to date knowledge of how Meta Ads, TikTok Ads, and other e-commerce advertising platforms work

- Strong analytical skills - you understand metrics and data-driven decision making

- Highly organized with excellent project management abilities



Bonus Points:

- Experience with AI tools for research, copywriting, or creative ideation is ideal, but we can share relevant workflows.

- Background in psychology, consumer behavior, or marketing 

- Experience working with Shopify brands

- Portfolio of successful creative campaigns with performance metrics

- Experience in health, wellness, beauty, or supplement niches



WHAT MAKES YOU PERFECT FOR THIS ROLE:



You're not just a copywriter or researcher - you're a strategic thinker who understands the entire customer journey. You can identify what makes people buy, translate that into compelling creative concepts, and work with creators and editors to bring those concepts to life. You thrive on performance data and love the challenge of continuous optimization.



WHAT WE OFFER:



- Stable Income: Fixed monthly salary

- Performance Bonuses: Earn extra based on creative performance metrics and brand growth. We grow because of your work? So does your role and compensation

- Creative Freedom: We value results, not micromanagement

- Long-Term Position: We're building a team for the long haul, not hiring for short-term projects



MPORTANT NOTES:



1. This is a full-time, long-term position - not project-based work

2. You must be comfortable working independently and taking ownership

3. We value quality and results over hours worked



Looking forward to finding the right person to join our team!

mbers worldwide and 200+ verified reviews with a 4.9 average rating.

s that get opened, read, and clicked
